title: Rigor, Vouch, Origin disclosure
description: Structural and vocabulary validation for the RIGOR.md frontmatter block. Semantic cross-field rules (level-to-checks consistency) are enforced separately by the semantic validator, not by this schema.

rigor.description: Human review/ownership level for the most recent significant changes, as a v0.2 level name. Tools also accept the legacy codes (R0-R4) and v0.1 names (none, surface) as input and normalize them to these names before validation.

vouch.description: Whether the author recommends depending on this code.

checks.description: Optional. The individual assurance checks behind the rigor level. Any subset may be present.

origin.description: Optional. The provenance trajectory: how the code was first authored and who maintains it now.

notes.description: Optional short free-text. The prose body below the frontmatter is the usual place for notes; this field exists for tools that prefer it inline.

checkValue.description: Done-states carry the actor: yes (unstated), human, ai, human-with-ai. Plus no and not-applicable. Strings, not booleans, to avoid YAML coercion footguns.
